transcript.title: Opening
transcript.text: Good morning Rushil Bhatia. Here is your daily news for Sunday, July 19. In today's briefing: top headlines, bhopal and madhya pradesh, business and markets, money, tax and personal finance, science and technology, development and policy, across india, around the world. Let's begin.

transcript.title: Top headlines
transcript.text: First, the stories leading the news this morning. US military death toll in Iran war rises to 16 after two troops killed in Jordan. Two additional US service members died defending against Iranian attacks in Jordan. This brings the total American military deaths in the conflict to sixteen. The ongoing conflict is largely fought through drones and missile strikes. Iranian authorities reported at least fifty people killed and over five hundred injured. Using abusive language, swearing don't count as obscenity under IPC: Supreme Court. Supreme Court clarified that abusive language is not obscenity under law. Mere swear words and profanities do not constitute an offense. The court set aside a conviction for using abusive slurs during an altercation. Obscenity requires a lascivious element appealing to prurient interests. With retirement rumours swirling, Rohit turns up for optional practice at Lord's. With retirement speculation growing and pressure mounting after a lean ODI series, Rohit Sharma attended India's optional practice session at Lord's. Former India captain looked in good touch as he prepared ahead of the series decider at Lord's. Social media influencers Andrew Tate, brother Tristan arrested in Miami. Federal authorities in Miami arrested Andrew and Tristan Tate on Saturday. The US Marshals Service confirmed their custody but did not reveal specific charges. An arrest warrant for the brothers remains sealed by the agency. The Tates are wanted in the United Kingdom for serious allegations. As Volkswagen puts 100 sheep to work, director says: Sheep project shows modern industry can. Volkswagen is using one hundred sheep to maintain its large solar farm in Poland. This initiative combines solar energy generation with active agriculture on the same land. The sheep grazing project demonstrates industry working in harmony with nature. Researchers are studying the impacts of solar infrastructure on animals and the land. Despite 35 minute delay Vikram-1 keeps date with Indian space history with Mission Aagaman success. SRIHARIKOTA: After some anxious moments when the automatic launch sequence of Vikram-1 was halted midway at just five minutes before lift-off, Skyroot Aerospace finally created history after it successfully placed India's first privately developed orbital-class rocket into space. Airbnb CEO says will keep investing in New York despite Mamdani's rent freeze plan. Airbnb and Anthropic are expanding their New York City operations significantly. This investment occurs despite concerns from business leaders about city policies. Airbnb purchased a building for its growing New York workforce. Anthropic will lease a large office building and double its staff. Human voices near salmon streams made bears and eagles flee, study finds. New findings from Canadian researchers reveal that human sounds near Alaska's salmon streams significantly disturb wildlife, leading to grizzly bears avoiding feeding areas. This interference disrupts the natural nutrient transfer to surrounding forests, emphasizing the detrimental effects of human activity on essential ecosystem dynamics. Scientists propose enhanced land management practices to safeguard these critical ecological processes. Scientists fit 75 pronghorns with GPS collars: What they found. GPS collars track pronghorn movement near a New Mexico solar project. Fences around the solar farm present a significant barrier to these animals. Pronghorn typically crawl under fences rather than jumping over them. Researchers suggest simple fence modifications could aid wildlife movement. Russia's 'Mnogotochie': The anti-drone bullet that splits into three after firing. Russia is supplying its forces with new Mnogotochie ammunition for drone interception. This specialized round splits into three projectiles after firing to widen the engagement pattern. The ammunition is compatible with standard Russian small arms and requires no modifications. It is available in two calibres and effective up to 300 meters against drones. 2 US service members killed, 1 missing after Iranian attack on Jordan base. Two US service members died and one is missing after an Iranian attack in Jordan. US and partner forces defended the base against Iranian missile and drone assaults. Iran warned the United States of "unforgettable lessons" if operations continued. US Central Command conducted strikes targeting Iranian surveillance and infrastructure sites.

transcript.title: Bhopal and Madhya Pradesh
transcript.text: Now, news closer to home in Bhopal and across Madhya Pradesh. Outstanding Performance By ALLEN Bhopal In NEET 2026. Students of ALLEN Career Institute, Bhopal, delivered an impressive performance in NEET UG 2026, with several securing ranks likely to ensure admission to government medical colleges. 2,000 Residents At Risk In Janata Quarters As Redevelopment Stalls In Bhopal. Around 2,000 residents living in nearly 600 dilapidated Janata Quarters in Bhopal remain at risk as the Housing Board's redevelopment project has stalled. Bhopal Central Vista: 10 Modern Towers Planned to Replace Ageing Government Buildings. The Madhya Pradesh Housing Board has advanced its rupees 1,000-crore Central Vista redevelopment project, with the master plan completed and the DPR under preparation. Donkeys Fed Gulab Jamun In Bhopal As Part Of 'Ritual' To Bring Rain. Donkeys fed gulab jamuns for rain in Bhopal, IMD forecasts wet spell in MP. Probe identifies four 'ghost' colleges in Bhopal. India Today impact: Government takes action after expose into 4 Madhya Pradesh colleges. From flicker to glitter: Iconic lakes sign to get a facelift. ReTiger found dead in Panna core, MP toll rises to 45. Bhopal may face water rationing as Upper Lake nears historic low, officials warn. BTech student dies by suicide. Speculation ends as Awadesh Nayak backs Congress ahead of Datia bypoll. MP's sarees, spices and handicrafts get ecommerce gateway to global markets. PW Vidyapeeth NEET UG 2026 Toppers: Indore, Bhopal and Gwalior Centres Shine with Top All India Ranks. Madhya Pradesh set to clear UCC draft with live-in registration provision. MP tops ASI list of antiquity thefts as Narwar cannon theft revives security concerns.

transcript.title: Business and markets
transcript.text: Moving to business, the economy and the markets. IPO GMP Check: SBI Funds Management IPO versus Alpine Texworld IPO - What grey market signals ahead of listings?. SBI Funds Management and Alpine Texworld IPOs closed their bidding on July 16, with SBI commanding a GMP of rupees 91 and being subscribed 41.66 times. Alpine Texworld had a GMP of rupees 1 and was subscribed 1.40 times, indicating differing market performances ahead of their listings. ICICI Bank Q1 Results 2026: Net profit rises 16 percent to rupees 14,804.50 crore. ICICI Bank reported a net profit of rupees 14,804.50 crore for Q1 2026, reflecting a 16 percent increase from rupees 12,768.21 crore in Q1 2025. The earnings announcement was made on July 18, 2026. Yes Bank Q1 results 2026: Net profit jumps 33.7 percent YoY, operating profit up 25.5 percent. Yes Bank Q1 results 2026: During the April to June 2026 period, Yes Bank reported net profit of rupees 1,071 crore, up 33.7 percent Y-o-Y and 0.2 percent Q-o-Q Axis Bank Q1 Results 2026: Net profit jumps 23 percent YoY to rupees 7,114 crore; NII rises 8 percent. Axis Bank reported strong Q1 FY27 earnings, with standalone net profit rising 22.5 percent to rupees 7,114 crore and net interest income growing over 8 percent to rupees 14,646 crore, surpassing Street estimates. MakeMyTrip IPO soon! Online travel giant files confidential DRHP with SEBI, 15 years after Nasdaq listing. MakeMyTrip has initiated the process for its India IPO by filing a Draft Red Herring Prospectus with SEBI. The offering involves an equity share sale by MakeMyTrip Limited and its subsidiary, ibibo Group, but details like the issue size remain undisclosed. Buy or sell: Sumeet Bagadia recommends three stocks to buy on Monday - 20 July 2026. Sumeet Bagadia of Choice Broking, highlighted that from a technical perspective, the index is now approaching the crucial resistance zone near 200-Day EMA, where a decisive breakout could trigger further upside momentum. Stocks to buy under rupees 200: Mehul Kothari of Anand Rathi recommends three shares to buy or sell. Mehul Kothari from Anand Rathi suggests a cautious approach for traders, recommending a decisive close above 24,400 for a bullish outlook. For stocks under rupees 200, he recommends Suzlon Energy, IDBI, and Zee Entertainment with specified buy prices and targets. NTA probe finds NEET OMR sheets tampered. The National Testing Agency found one disputed NEET OMR sheet was AI-generated. Axis Bank Q1 preview: Profit, NII to stay healthy; NIM likely to shrink due to deposit repricing, competitive funding. As Axis Bank gears up to announce its Q1 results, analysts predict significant growth in net interest income and net profit. However, a potential decline in net interest margin due to competitive pressures and deposit repricing raises concerns. Amarnath, Vaishno Devi Yatras suspended. Authorities have temporarily suspended the Amarnath and Mata Vaishno Devi Yatras from July 19 following an IMD forecast of widespread rainfall across Jammu and Kashmir. ICICI Bank Q1 results preview: Net profit seen up 3.1 percent YoY to rupees 13,164 crore; NIMs, asset quality may remain stable. ICICI Bank Q1 results preview: ICICI Bank's loan growth in the June quarter is expected to be healthy at 18.5 percent YoY, led by gold loans, corporate loans, personal loans and mortgages. Deposits are seen rising 15.2 percent YoY. National Film Awards : Check complete winners list. The winners of the 72nd National Film Awards are being announced in New Delhi, celebrating the best of Indian cinema across feature films, non-feature films and writing on cinema for films certified in 2024. HDFC Bank Q1 results preview: Net profit likely to rise 6 percent YoY on strong loan growth, NIMs to remain flat. HDFC Bank Q1 results preview: HDFC Bank Q1 results are expected to be mixed, with NIM likely to remain largely stable sequentially, while asset quality is expected to remain steady. Analysts anticipate healthy double-digit loan growth, supported by strong business momentum. UK Court orders Raj Kundra to pay $4.94 mn. The dispute related to a 11.7 percent stake that Kundra's Kuki Investments Ltd held in Mauritius-based EM Sporting Holdings, the EMV subsidiary that controlled the IPL franchise. Q1 results 2026: HDFC Bank to ICICI Bank, Axis Bank among over 20 companies to declare Q1 earnings today; full list here. On July 18, over 20 companies, primarily banks, will announce their Q1 FY27 earnings, providing crucial insights into India's banking sector. Key indicators include profit growth and asset quality, with strong focus on management commentary amid rising geopolitical tensions. Nava Raipur's smart city dream runs into hurdles. Despite being envisioned as Chhattisgarh's smart city, Nava Raipur continues to grapple with poor last-mile connectivity, unreliable app-based deliveries and limited public transport. Why Netflix's AI push isn't reviving the stock. The streamer says that gen-AI workflows now have been used in about 300 of the company's titles. Messi's World Cup greatness, one record at a time. The spotlight will be firmly on Argentina captain Lionel Messi, who is expected to play the final World Cup match of his career. These three large-cap stocks look cheap. Here's why investors should look closer. These three large-cap stocks trade at relatively low valuations versus peers, but investors should look beyond cheap P/E multiples before making a decision. Government extends renewable energy exemption. The government has extended the exemption for net-metering and open access RE projects. Wangchuk's doctor questions Hospital's reports. Activist Sonam Wangchuk's doctor expressed suspicion regarding hospital medical reports. US reaches H-1B visa cap for FY2027. The US has reached its H-1B visa cap for Fiscal Year 2027, with USCIS confirming it received enough petitions to fill all 85,000 available slots. Vikram-1 launch delayed 35 mins due to glitch. Explaining the reason behind the initial delay, Narayanan said the problem occurred during the transition from the ground segment to the onboard computer. Om Birla allows separate seating for rebel TMC MPs. Lok Sabha Speaker Om Birla has accepted the request of 20 rebel Trinamool Congress (TMC) MPs for separate seating in the Lok Sabha.

transcript.title: Money, tax and personal finance
transcript.text: Now to your money — taxes, savings and personal finance. Employee wrongly reported rupees 65.21 lakh VRS payout in ITR, lost tax relief; ITAT Pune rules the amount is not taxable and grants him relief. Employee got rupees 65.21 lakh as VRS after company shut plant, but wrong ITR reporting led to tax dispute; ITAT Pune rules in his favour and gives him relief from tax. Know how this empoyee won the case in ITAT Pune. NPS onboarding redesign, AI-powered pension advisory platform, NPS Swasthya, PFRDA Chair encourages 'responsible' AI use. PFRDA Chairman S Ramann outlined an AI roadmap to redesign the National Pension Scheme's onboarding process. He also encouraged 'responsible' AI use for a pension advisory platform powered by the technology. These property sellers may need to re-compute advance tax after delayed release of Cost Inflation Index for tax year 2026-27; here's what to do. Property sellers may need to re-compute advance tax after the Cost Inflation Index release. This delay impacts individuals selling property acquired before July 23, 2024. Advance tax payments for the first quarter were due before the index was notified. Underestimating tax liability could lead to interest charges for deferred payments. Foreign asset details for 2022-24 now available under AIS: What to do if you missed disclosing them in previous ITRs?. Taxpayers can now view their foreign assets and income via the AIS on the income tax e-filing portal. The AIS currently includes data for 2022-2024, with 2025 data expected by late 2026. Here's what to do if you missed disclosing them in previous IT rupees Children can not have birthright to father's self-acquired property even if he got it via gift, Will, family arrangement or partition in this case; Know what Mitakshara Hindu law says. A recent ruling clarifies children's birthright in inherited property. Mitakshara Hindu law states self-acquired property does not grant automatic birthrights. The source of the father's title determines if property is ancestral or self-acquired. Children can still inherit self-acquired property through succession laws or a Will. ITR filing 2026: FD interest is taxable , here's how to report it correctly. FD interest income is fully taxable but tax implications may vary based on the chosen tax regime and taxpayer age. Some deductions may also be available, based on your age and income profile. Here's all you need to know. Name appearing in builder's records showing cash payment: Will you need to pay income tax for such property purchase? Here's what ITAT said. Can the Income Tax Department tax you based only on papers found during a builder's search? A recent ITAT ruling explains when third-party documents are not enough and the important rights every property buyer should know. Income-tax returns: Check penalty for missing deadline for filing ITR , Key things to know. ITR: Taxpayers must file their income-tax returns for FY26 / AY27 by 31 August this year, extended from 30 June. Here's how much penalty you can be charged and deadline for delayed ITR filing during the tax year. Berkshire has 30 percent of its portfolio in Apple, Alphabet: Expert says not 'appropriate strategy' for retail investors but. Berkshire Hathaway now allocates nearly 30 percent of its $351 billion portfolio to Apple and Alphabet, reflecting a shift in Warren Buffett's investment strategy. UPI MDR may return for large merchants: What it means for your digital payments. India's government is considering a small Merchant Discount Rate for large UPI retailers. This potential change aims to create a sustainable revenue model for payment providers. Large merchants with significant annual turnover would face this minor transaction fee. Peer-to-peer and small merchant transactions will remain free of any charges. EPFO: Here's how to link your Aadhaar with UAN using the Umang app , Stepwise guide. EPFO: Aadhaar linking is mandatory for several EPF services. Today, we check how EPF subscribers can link Aadhaar with their Universal Account Number (UAN) using the government's Umang mobile app. Claiming tax relief on salary arrears, advance pay or gratuity? Here's why Form 10e must be filed before ITR. Salary arrears and lump-sum payments can increase tax liability. Taxpayers must file Form 10E before their ITR to claim relief under Section 89 of the Income-tax Act. Here's what you need to know. FD rate up to 8.05 percent for senior citizens investing for five years; Know list of banks. Some banks offer senior citizens fixed deposits with interest rates up to 8.05 percent. These deposits are available for a five-year term and up to rupees 3 crore. Banks deduct tax at source if FD interest exceeds rupees 1 lakh annually. Senior citizens can submit Form 15H to avoid TDS if their tax liability is zero. 8th pay commission: Basic pay, allowances , components of salary structure in focus; Here's why. Around 50 lakh central government employees and about 65 lakh retired central government pensioners, including defence personnel and retirees, will benefit from the recommendations across 18 employee levels. Lost job or retiring voluntarily with rupees 1 crore? Here's how to get maximum long-lasting monthly income from your savings. Financial planning for retirement: Early retirement necessitates careful financial planning for extended periods. A rupees 1 crore corpus may prove insufficient for early retirees in India. Experts suggest a diversified investment strategy for long-term financial security. This approach balances regular income with growth to combat inflation. Dearness Allowance: From DA hikes to pay arrears and other key announcements , Check latest updates, FAQ answers here. About 1 crore beneficiaries , close to 50 lakh central government employees and nearly 65 lakh retired central government pensioners, including defence personnel and retirees , will benefit from an increase in DA and DR. Saturday bank holiday: Are banks open or closed today on July 18, 2026? Check banking services you can avail. Bank holiday today: Customers who had planned to visit their local bank branch for banking services such as cash deposits, cheque clearances, demand drafts or updating passbooks are left wondering if they should go to complete their tasks today.s. ITR filing 2026: If your tax liability is nil after Section 87A rebate, should you still file a return?. Even with a zero tax liability due to the Section 87A rebate, filing an income tax return (ITR) may be necessary. Your obligation to file depends on your total income and the tax regime you choose. Sold a property in FY 2025-26? Why paying the flat 12.5 percent LTCG tax without indexation could save you more. Many property sellers may end up paying more tax under the 20 percent tax regime with indexation than under the flat 12.5 percent LTCG tax. The benefit of indexation depends on how much the property's value has appreciated over time, making it important to compare both tax calculations before filing. From Ketan Parekh scam to Covid: What 25 years of history reveals about ideal asset allocation during market downturns. A portfolio invested entirely in equities typically suffers the deepest drawdowns, whereas adding debt investments can substantially reduce losses during market corrections. AMFI updates large, mid, and small-cap stock lists in July 2026: Which stocks climbed the ladder and which fell?. AMFI's latest market-cap review has reshuffled several stocks across the large, mid and small-cap categories. While companies such as BSE, BHEL and Vodafone Idea moved up, others including Hero MotoCorp and Dr. Reddy's Laboratories slipped to lower categories based on average market capitalisation. AMFI eases mutual fund transmission process: Easier address, name, and signature verification after the investor's death. The move is intended to address operational challenges frequently faced by families during the transmission process while ensuring a smoother and more uniform experience across mutual fund houses. 8th Pay Commission salary calculator: Can Level 4-6 employees get up to rupees 27,290/month HRA? See estimates at 2.0, 2.1, 2.28 and 2.57 fitment factors. The 8th Pay Commission is working on revising the salaries of central government employees, and the increase may not be limited to basic pay alone. Are you 35 or older? Here's why life insurance deserves a place in your financial portfolio. After age 35, financial responsibilities typically increase, making a balanced portfolio more important. Experts recommend combining life insurance with equity investments, mutual funds and SIPs to build long-term wealth, protect family finances, manage liabilities and boost overall financial security.

transcript.title: Science and technology
transcript.text: Next, science and technology. Dave Eggers told OpenAI staff that ChatGPT was 'silencing an entire generation'. Last year, Sam Altman invited author Dave Eggers to give a talk to around 200 OpenAI staffers. The man has written countless novels, screenplays, pieces of journalism, started McSweeney's, and founded multiple schools and nonprofits that support writers and the arts more broadly. So one might expect he'd roll into the company's offices and offer Waymo says San Francisco service has resumed after one-hour pause. This isn't the first time power outages have caused issues for Waymo. Intermittent fasting helped people keep weight off for a year. A 12-week intermittent fasting program produced weight-loss benefits that were still visible a year later. Participants who ate within an eight-hour window maintained more weight loss than those who followed their usual longer eating schedule. Both early and late eating windows worked, while early fasting appeared especially helpful for preserving fat loss. Google might not kneecap the Pixel 11a with an old processor. Mystic Leaks suggests that the Pixel 11a will return to featuring a flagship-grade processor with the Tensor G6. Rather than the Tensor G5 found in the Pixel 10 and 10 Pro, the Pixel 10a shipped with the previous generation Tensor G4. That was a huge disappointment since, typically, the Pixel a lineup kept the modern Kimi: Threat or menace?. Chinese company Moonshot AI released a new version of its Kimi model this week, prompting concern about "full AI communism." Google is open-sourcing its 3D emoji. Now, if you want to, you can use Google's 3D emoji in your own creations. The company shared some details about how it went about designing the little pictograms and why, as part of World Emoji Day on Friday. Things you might not necessarily worry about in a 2D illustration suddenly become very important when All the EVs that were discontinued or killed off in the U.S. this year. Th Honda Prologue will no longer be sold in the U.S., joining a growing list of EV models to exit the market this year. Popular sugar substitutes linked to faster brain aging. Several popular sugar substitutes may not be as harmless as they seem. Adults who consumed the most artificial sweeteners showed substantially faster declines in memory and thinking, especially if they were under 60 or had diabetes. The highest intake was linked to cognitive aging roughly 1.6 years faster than the lowest intake. Researchers stressed that more studies are needed before concluding that sweeteners are the cause. GoPro's discounted Max 2 bundle includes $100 worth of accessories. A 360-degree camera is a great way to ensure you capture every bit of the action, but prices tend to be on the high end for models worth your attention. That's why it's notable that the GoPro Max 2 accessory bundle is discounted to $369 at Amazon (a dollar more at Best Buy and GoPro). Federal employees can download TikTok on their work phones again. The Department of Justice says that federal employees can now download TikTok on their government devices. Earth's biggest volcanic event transformed an entire oceanic plate. Seismic waves have revealed that the oceanic plate beneath the Ontong Java Plateau was dramatically transformed by the colossal volcanic activity that created it more than 100 million years ago. Researchers found a complex structure of horizontal layers cut through by vast swarms of vertical magma channels, along with unusually slow seismic waves suggesting that deep-rising magma chemically altered the plate itself. The Guardian's Carter Sherman fondly remembers being terrified by Ocarina of Time. Carter Sherman has been covering sex, gender, and the complex personal and national politics that accompany them for years. She was a senior reporter for Vice and has written for Elle, Ms. magazine, and Los Angeles magazine as well. Along the way, she's garnered a Scripps Howard Award, a National Press Club Journalism Award, and A 600-mile road trip (and data) proves EV charging doesn't suck anymore. A recent road trip in an EV revealed just how much faster and more reliable DC Fast charging has become in the U.S. Surprise! Facial recognition smart locks are actually good. Hands-free unlocking is the future of smart locks. The best smart home tech removes friction, and having your door unlock for you as you walk up is as frictionless as it gets - no passcodes to remember, no need to have a free hand to wave, press, or poke at the lock. One way to Neil Rimer thinks the AI money is coming back out. Neil Rimer, the venture capitalist who co-founded Index Ventures, predicts the historic wealth AI is generating in Silicon Valley will have to be redistributed, voluntarily or involuntarily. Mediterranean diet may activate tiny proteins that protect the heart and brain. The Mediterranean diet may influence aging through tiny proteins produced inside the cell's mitochondria. Older adults who followed the diet most closely had higher levels of humanin and SHMOOSE, which have been linked to heart and brain protection. Olive oil, fish, legumes, and fewer refined carbohydrates appeared especially important. The findings could eventually help scientists develop more personalized nutrition plans for healthy aging. Sony's flagship RGB LED TV is incredible. The Sony Bravia 9 II is the most anticipated new TV in years. It's an amazing RGB LED TV. I watched Dungeons and Dragons: Honor Among Thieves on the new Bravia with my son, who has been getting into the roleplaying game but had never seen the movie. The landscapes of Faerûn looked natural and NASA's James Webb catches a supermassive black hole feeding. JWST has captured unusually detailed images of gas feeding the supermassive black hole at the center of NGC 4696. A vast filament appears to funnel material into an 800-light-year-wide spinning disk, where gas races around at up to 600 kilometers per second. The findings suggest black holes may recycle their own fuel by heating gas with jets and later drawing the cooled material back in. More games should be on rails (literally). It's been a good few weeks for games on rails. Nintendo's Star Fox remake wisely kept the tightly scripted, action-packed levels from Star Fox 64 largely the same, and they're still fun to fly through nearly 20 years later. Denshattack!, a new game from Undercoders, similarly features levels packed with carefully orchestrated sequences to great A shattered asteroid may have bombarded Earth 800 million years ago. A catastrophic asteroid breakup may have triggered a huge wave of impacts across the inner solar system about 800 million years ago. The debris was launched from near a gravitational gateway controlled by Jupiter, sending fragments toward Earth, the Moon, and Mars. The bombardment may explain ancient lunar craters and could have contributed to major climate and biological changes on Earth. The apps, gadgets, and tools every reader needs. Hi, friends! Welcome to Installer No. 136, your guide to the best and Verge-iest stuff in the world. (If you're new here, welcome, hope your neighborhood isn't as smoky as mine, and also you can read all the old editions at the Installer homepage.) This week, I've been recording the next season of Version History Fine, electric mountain bikes don't suck. Cheater, I'd grumble between huffs as yet another e-bike rider casually skittered past me on a steep ascent. It's this purist attitude that, for years, has left me blind to one simple fact: electric mountain bikes are fun! My attitude adjustment came a few weeks ago, the very first time I rode an Amflow PX "Silly sprinklers" help scientists finally solve Feynman's famous sprinkler mystery. A team of mathematicians used whimsical "silly sprinklers" to solve a physics mystery that has puzzled scientists for decades. Their experiments showed that the rotation of both normal and reverse sprinklers is driven by the momentum of flowing water, not by the outside water flow or other long-standing theories. The results finally provide a clear answer to Feynman's famous sprinkler problem. They could also help engineers design more efficient fluid-powered machines.

transcript.title: Development and policy
transcript.text: Now to development, infrastructure and government policy. In 1991, India's foreign exchange reserves had fallen so low the government secretly airlifted 47 tonnes of gold abroad as collateral for an emergency loan, and within a decade the same economy would go on to become a software exporting giant. India, Estonia eye deeper cooperation in digital technology, manufacturing to strengthen India-EU partnershi. Uttarakhand halts felling of 3,000 trees for Dehradun-Rishikesh highway project amid protests. Supreme Court's 'Delhi Metro' Judgment Caused Extensive Damage To Arbitration In India: Justice Ujjal. New Delhi Air Pollution Index Further Pushes for EV Adoption in India. PM Modi's Jalandhar Visit Reflects India's Growing Confidence in Building the Future: Dr. Ashok Kumar Mittal. U.S. lawmaker urges Congress to review arms sales to India. Buddha's Wisdom and India's Roadmap for a New International Order. Modern Rail infrastructure will accelerate Developed India vision: Dhami. Piyush Goyal addresses India-Estonia Business Forum, calls for deeper ties.

transcript.title: Across India
transcript.text: Turning to the big national stories. Skyroot puts first private rocket in orbit and India in elite club; Vikram-1 delivers 6 payloads after 35-minute delay. Skyroot Aerospace's Vikram-1 rocket successfully launched into low Earth orbit on Saturday. This historic mission placed six payloads into their targeted orbit successfully. India now becomes the third nation with private orbital launch capability. The Hyderabad-based startup achieved this significant milestone on its first attempt. Another House panel defers edu reforms report adoption; move seen as part of govt's outreach to oppn for delimit bill support. Two parliamentary committees have postponed approving their reports on significant legislation. This development occurs as the government seeks opposition support for other political proposals. The anti-graft bill and higher education regulator bills face widespread protest. Both bills may not be presented during the current parliamentary session. Skyroot's orbital launch: India's own SpaceX is born, startup to give country another option to Isro for satellite launches. Skyroot Aerospace successfully launched India's first privately developed orbital-class rocket, Vikram-1. This historic flight reached low Earth orbit and deployed six payloads for various applications. The launch signifies a major step for India's private space sector and its global market aspirations. Other Indian space startups are expected to accelerate their orbital rocket programs following this success. rupees 70,000 cr investments proposed for def corridors in UP, TN; rupees 10,000 crore already invested: Rajnath. Defence Minister Rajnath Singh highlighted India's strong defence preparedness and self-reliance. Advanced systems like Akash Teer and BrahMos were effectively used during Operation Sindoor. Defence industrial corridors in UP and Tamil Nadu are fostering indigenous manufacturing and global integration. Positive indigenisation lists and a new Defence Acquisition Procedure are accelerating domestic production. I was discouraged from taking bold decision to privatise space sector, today you proved me right: PM Modi to Skyroot after Vikram-1 successful launch. India achieved a historic milestone with the successful launch of its first private orbital rocket. Prime Minister Narendra Modi lauded the achievement, emphasizing faith in the nation's youth. This success positions India as the third country with indigenous private orbital launch capabilities. Skyroot Aerospace's Vikram-1 rocket carried a special message for the universe. 'Entire BJP trembling with fear': Prashant Kishor on Bankipur assembly bypoll. Jan Suraaj Party founder Prashant Kishor claims the BJP fears his electoral debut. He alleges the ruling party is now campaigning door-to-door for votes. Kishor urges voters to reject money and food distribution practices. The Jan Suraaj Party aims to offer a credible political alternative. Mehbooba rejects Farooq's invite to join Jantar Mantar protest; Engineer Rashid to walk barefoot in solidarity. SRINAGAR: J and K chief minister Omar Abdullah and NC president Farooq Abdullah, along with party MLAs, left for New Delhi Saturday to hold a protest at Jantar Mantar on July 20 for restoration of statehood, even though police permission for the sit-in was yet to come. CBSE declares Class 10 second edition board results; nearly 60 percent candidates improve performance. NEW DELHI: The Central Board on Secondary Education (CBSE) on Saturday declared the results for second edition of the class 10th board examinations. 'Ayodhya verdict not fully implemented': Nirmohi Akhara moves Supreme Court, seeks reconstitution of Ram Temple trust. NEW DELHI: The Nirmohi Akhara has moved the Supreme Court seeking directions for what it calls the "proper implementation" of a Constitution bench's November 2019 Ayodhya judgment that led to the cobstruction of the Ram Temple in Ayodhya. US new immigration rule threatens stay of over 3 lakh Indian students on F-1 visa. Experts say new rule will not only impact F-1 status but also affect optional practical training Bihar government faces first pushback from within: BJP and JDU leaders say don't change education structure. In a memorandum to CM, they oppose bid to decouple undergraduate colleges from their parent state universities J and K Police order SIT probe into Doda youth's death amid protests, shutdown. While police have not issued a detailed statement on the circumstances surrounding Hussain's death, sources said the incident occurred after police set up a checkpoint following a tip-off about alleged bovine smuggling. 'If Modi doesn't sack Dharmendra Pradhan, he is responsible too': CJP founder Abhijeet Dipke. Speaking to The Indian Express, Cockroach Janta Party founder Abhijeet Dipke accused the Centre of suppressing a peaceful movement and alleged that Wangchuk had been "illegally detained". 'Stripped and beaten for 4 hours' inside school hostel, Dalit student recalls ordeal. Police file FIR against six students, who targeted the youth over allegations of stealing a phone and money. Railways to develop Rishikesh as feeder station to reduce pressure on Haridwar, boost train capacity. Rishikesh railway station will be upgraded as a feeder station to decongest Haridwar, improving train operations and passenger capacity. 'Violence a core tenet of Modi govt': Rahul Gandhi condemns Sonam Wangchuk's removal from Jantar Mantar. Gandhi said the paper leaks, the rising cost of education and student suicides were among the most important issues affecting the country's future. Donation theft at Badrinath Dham: CCTV reveals how accused operated, even saffron wasn't spared. Police said that the SIT conducted a detailed analysis of CCTV footage from the thali bhent counting room across multiple dates and recorded statements of witnesses.

transcript.title: Around the world
transcript.text: And finally, here is what is happening around the world. Hungary's president agrees to stand down after parliament backs removal. Tamás Sulyok, seen as an Orbán loyalist, had until Saturday night to agree to end his term. Many Ukrainian soldiers outraged over removal of defence minister, troops tell BBC. Protests erupted in Ukraine on Thursday after Mykhailo Fedorov's removal - and now soldiers are also criticising the move. Space start-up launches India's first commercial rocket designed for orbit. India's first space-tech unicorn Skyroot Aerospace successfully launched Vikram-1 on Saturday. Indian activist on hunger strike for 20 days forcibly taken to hospital. Cockroach Janta Party founder Abhijeet Dipke has now begun an indefinite fast in Sonam Wangchuk's place. White House defends Argentina team over Falklands banner. The comments could further fuel the row over the incident, which has seen Downing Street back calls for Fifa to investigate. One anti-war critic fined, another held as Russia clamps down on dissent. While Boris Nadezhdin is barred from running for parliament, blogger Ilya Remeslo is remanded in custody. Kim Jong Un was meant to be their only idol - then North Koreans discovered K-pop. Defectors tell the BBC that despite restrictions, K-pop has cut through in the stifling dictatorship. He made history in Congress - then the Supreme Court changed its mind. Residents say they're afraid that if Shomari Figures loses his seat, their federal-funding could disappear with him. How these twins' rare growth disorder could provide the key to preventing cancer. Researchers have found that people with Laron syndrome have a lower incidence of cancer than the wider population. Helicopters dump water as huge Norway fire destroys homes. The blaze has destroyed more than 100 homes in Drammen and has spread to a nearby forest, authorities say. Saka hat-trick helps England down France to win World Cup 2026 Bronze Final. Kylian Mbappe scores twice for France to hit World Cup record, but England win 6-4 in third-place playoff final.

transcript.title: Sign-off
transcript.text: That is your briefing. Stay sharp, and have a strong day.
